laws.title: Public Law 14-90 | Presidential Suck Up Act
laws.status_basis: Source is intact and operative on its face, but SEC 2(a)(i) (Acting Waiver for U.S. Ambassadors) is expressly repealed by PL 15-102 (New Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act) SEC II(b), which is itself in this batch. Remainder (SEC 2 acting-waiver for other offices, SEC 1 short title, SEC 3 establishment) is not shown repealed by the supplied set. Manifest 'Repealed Laws' list label is unreliable; the source shows only a partial express repeal by a later act. Using 'partially-current' semantics.

provisions.text_summary: Short title: 'Presidential Suck Up Act'
provisions.evidence: "This act shall be referred to as the “Presidential Suckup Act”"
provisions.notes: Note-worthy only.

provisions.text_summary: Individuals nominated to listed offices (Ambassadors, Dept. Asst. Secretaries, Dept. Inspectors General, Service Secretaries, U.S. Marshal, Deputy Directors) may act while awaiting Senate confirmation
provisions.exact_change: Acting-service authority pending confirmation; subclause (i) Ambassadors later repealed by PL 15-102
provisions.evidence: "Individuals nominated to the following offices will be allowed to act in their capacity whilst waiting confirmation from the United States Senate"
provisions.notes: General-law vacancies/acting-service matter; closest anchor executive/appointments. (i) Ambassadors superseded.

provisions.text_summary: Nominees who fail Senate lose acting capacity; re-nominees may not act
provisions.exact_change: Limitation on acting service after Senate rejection
provisions.evidence: "If such nominated individuals fail the United States Senate they shall no longer act in such capacity"

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately on passage and signature
provisions.evidence: "This bill will take effect immediately after passage through both houses of Congress and a signature from the President"

current_implementation.detail: Note declares 'Repealed or superseded; retained solely as statutory history' and boilerplate codification prose; source is actually a partially-current general acting-service statute. Trello link present. No quoted-content dump. Note fails to record the partial repeal by PL 15-102 of subclause (i) (Ambassadors).

recommended_actions: Replace boilerplate note at /us/usc/t3/s1 with an honest statutory note describing the acting-service authority; record that SEC 2(a)(i) (Ambassadors) was repealed by PL 15-102 §II(b)
Remove Trello link
Do not create permanent Code section text; keep as statutory note under title 3 (or title 5 appointments material)

laws.title: Public Law 14-91 | Uniform Code of Military Justice Reform Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The Act is a shell: it declares a short title, definitions, severability, findings, and directs that a 'newly proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice' be 'immediately applied to all branches,' but the actual substantive UCMJ text is NOT contained in the source. SEC 2 says the short title 'shall refer to the “Proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice”.' No determinable operative text is supplied. Cannot lawfully codify content that is not in the source.

provisions.text_summary: Short title: 'Uniform Code of Military Justice Reform Act of 2023'
provisions.evidence: "This act shall be referred to as “Uniform Code of Military Justice Reform Act of 2023”"

provisions.text_summary: Defines the Act to refer to the 'Proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice'
provisions.exact_change: None determinable; the referenced Proposed UCMJ text is absent from the source
provisions.evidence: "...shall refer to the “ Proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice”"
provisions.notes: source-defect-nondeterminable

provisions.text_summary: Severability; anti-supersession clause
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable..."

provisions.text_summary: Findings on importance of periodic UCMJ review
provisions.evidence: "It is found that the Uniform Code of Military Justice is an essential component..."
provisions.notes: Duplicate SEC numbering in source (two SEC 5).

provisions.text_summary: Directs immediate application of the proposed UCMJ to all Armed Forces branches
provisions.exact_change: Directive references an external 'proposed UCMJ' not in the source; nondeterminable
provisions.evidence: "the newly proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice shall be immediately applied to all branches of the U.S Armed Forces"
provisions.notes: source-defect-nondeterminable

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately on passage and signature
provisions.evidence: "This act shall immediately take effect upon passage of both chambers..."

current_implementation.detail: Note has a full-text quoted-content dump (has_quoted_content_dump true, sha reference present) and Trello link. Declared status 'Current or not wholly repealed' is defensible in the loose sense, but the codification prose is boilerplate. Because the substantive UCMJ is absent, nothing determinable should be codified as permanent text.

recommended_actions: Remove the full-text quoted-content dump and Trello link from note rp-pl014091-codification

Replace with a concise statutory note at title 10 recording the short title, findings, and severability, and flag that the referenced 'Proposed Uniform Code of Military Justice' text was not supplied (source-defect-nondeterminable)
Do NOT invent UCMJ section text

provisions.text_summary: Appointment/removal of IGs; 48-hour congressional notice; Deputy IG; Assistant IGs

provisions.text_summary: Effective on simple-majority passage and signature unless veto overridden
provisions.evidence: "Will be declared law once passed with a simple majority by both chambers..."

laws.title: Public Law 15-108 | The Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: Source intact and operative. Content is a set of internal congressional document-labeling rules (bills, resolutions, concurrent/joint resolutions, amendments, committee reports, treaty documents). No self-repeal or sunset. Manifest 'amend/insert/repeal' operative-language hits are false positives from the descriptive text about legislative procedure, NOT amendments to a Code section. Current, but non-Code (internal congressional rules).

provisions.text_summary: Prescribes labeling conventions for Bills (H.R.#/S.#), Resolutions, Concurrent Resolutions, Joint Resolutions, Amendments, and miscellaneous items (committee reports H.Rep.(C)-#, treaty documents)
provisions.evidence: "H.R.# and S.# shall be only used when bills are being proposed."
provisions.notes: Internal rules of Congress; ordinarily excluded from positive-law Code sections. Keep as note if useful.

laws.title: Public Law 15-111 | Presidential Transition Protection Act
laws.status_basis: Source intact and fully operative; creates a criminal prohibition on obstructing a presidential transition with defined penalties. No self-repeal/sunset; anti-supersession clause. Nothing in the set repeals it. Current.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability/anti-supersession, effective date
provisions.evidence: "cited as the “Presidential Transition Protection Act”."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of presidential transition, president-elect, and obstruction
provisions.evidence: "Obstruction of a Presidential Transition shall be defined as: an act that involves influencing, impending, or otherwise interfering with a presidential transitional process."

provisions.text_summary: Prohibits obstruction of the peaceful transfer of power; penalty 5-7 days imprisonment and/or fine up to $1,000; no bar from future office
provisions.exact_change: New criminal offense (obstruction of presidential transition) with penalties
provisions.evidence: "Any individual accused of obstruction of a Presidential Transition may face a minimum sentence of five (5) days imprisonment to a maximum sentence of seven (7) days imprisonment, a fine no higher than one thousand dollars ($1,000) or both."
provisions.notes: Criminal-offense subject matter better anchored at title 18 or title 3 (presidential transition).

current_implementation.detail: Placement at title 49 (transportation) is clearly wrong for a presidential-transition criminal-offense statute; title 3 (President) or title 18 (crimes) fits. Declared 'Current' is right; codification prose is boilerplate. Manifest lists a Trello link in the note metadata (trello_links populated) though the note field here shows dump false.

recommended_actions: Re-anchor from t49 to title 3 (presidential transition) and/or title 18 (criminal offense)
Replace boilerplate note with an honest statutory note of the offense and penalty
Remove Trello link if present
Keep as statutory note; the short prohibition could alternatively support a concise new criminal provision

laws.title: Public Law 16-114 | Amended Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act
laws.status_basis: Source intact and operative. Its sole substantive act is to amend PL 15-102 (New Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act, in this batch) SEC III(e), quoting the old text and providing exact replacement text. Nothing repeals PL 16-114. Current (an amending statute).

provisions.evidence: "cited as the “Amended Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act”."

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 15-102 SEC III(e): replaces prohibition on creating new regional ambassadorships with authority for the President alone to create posts (with Senate notification/consent)
provisions.exact_change: Strike SEC III(e) ('The President, Secretary of State or any individual is prohibited from creating more regional ambassadorships posts...') and insert 'The President is the only individual permitted to create more regional ambassadorships posts if they deem it necessary. The President is obligated to notify the United States Senate of such creations. Any individual appointed to newly created regional ambassadorships shall receive the consent of the United States Senate before fulfilling their duties.'
provisions.evidence: "Section III, Subsection e shall now read the following: ... The President is the only individual permitted to create more regional ambassadorships posts..."
provisions.notes: In-batch target (PL 15-102); exact strike/insert text is determinable.
